# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { SignJWT, jwtVerify, JWTPayload } from "jose";

// Configuration constants
const PUBLIC_PATHS = new Set([
  "/",
  "/login",
  "/register",
  "/api/auth/login",
  "/api/auth/register",
  "/api/auth/refresh",
  "/_next",
  "/favicon.ico",
]);

const STATIC_ASSET_PREFIXES = ["/_next/", "/static/", "/images/", "/fonts/"];

const AUTH_COOKIE_NAME = "__Host-auth-token";
const REFRESH_COOKIE_NAME = "__Host-refresh-token";
const LOGIN_PATH = "/login";
const TOKEN_ISSUER = "nextjs-edge-auth";
const TOKEN_AUDIENCE = "nextjs-app";

// Rate limiting store (in-memory, per edge instance)
const rateLimitStore = new Map<string, { count: number; resetTime: number }>();
const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 60_000; // 1 minute
const RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS = 100;

function getSecretKey(): Uint8Array {
  const secret = process.env.AUTH_SECRET;
  if (!secret || secret.length < 32) {
    throw new Error(
      "AUTH_SECRET environment variable must be set and at least 32 characters long"
    );
  }
  return new TextEncoder().encode(secret);
}

function getRefreshSecretKey(): Uint8Array {
  const secret = process.env.AUTH_REFRESH_SECRET;
  if (!secret || secret.length < 32) {
    throw new Error(
      "AUTH_REFRESH_SECRET environment variable must be set and at least 32 characters long"
    );
  }
  return new TextEncoder().encode(secret);
}

interface AuthTokenPayload extends JWTPayload {
  sub: string;
  email: string;
  role: string;
  sessionId: string;
}

async function verifyAuthToken(
  token: string
): Promise<AuthTokenPayload | null> {
  try {
    // Validate token format before attempting verification
    if (!token || typeof token !== "string" || token.length > 4096) {
      return null;
    }

    // Basic JWT format check (three base64url segments)
    const parts = token.split(".");
    if (parts.length !== 3) {
      return null;
    }

    const { payload } = await jwtVerify(token, getSecretKey(), {
      issuer: TOKEN_ISSUER,
      audience: TOKEN_AUDIENCE,
      algorithms: ["HS256"],
      maxTokenAge: "15m",
      clockTolerance: 5,
    });

    // Validate required claims exist and are proper types
    if (
      !payload.sub ||
      typeof payload.sub !== "string" ||
      !payload.email ||
      typeof (payload as AuthTokenPayload).email !== "string" ||
      !payload.role ||
      typeof (payload as AuthTokenPayload).role !== "string" ||
      !(payload as AuthTokenPayload).sessionId ||
      typeof (payload as AuthTokenPayload).sessionId !== "string"
    ) {
      return null;
    }

    // Validate email format
    const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
    if (!emailRegex.test((payload as AuthTokenPayload).email)) {
      return null;
    }

    // Validate role against allowlist
    const allowedRoles = new Set(["user", "admin", "moderator"]);
    if (!allowedRoles.has((payload as AuthTokenPayload).role)) {
      return null;
    }

    // Validate sub format (should be a UUID or similar safe identifier)
    const uuidRegex =
      /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i;
    if (!uuidRegex.test(payload.sub)) {
      return null;
    }

    return payload as AuthTokenPayload;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

async function verifyRefreshToken(
  token: string
): Promise<AuthTokenPayload | null> {
  try {
    if (!token || typeof token !== "string" || token.length > 4096) {
      return null;
    }

    const parts = token.split(".");
    if (parts.length !== 3) {
      return null;
    }

    const { payload } = await jwtVerify(token, getRefreshSecretKey(), {
      issuer: TOKEN_ISSUER,
      audience: TOKEN_AUDIENCE,
      algorithms: ["HS256"],
      maxTokenAge: "7d",
      clockTolerance: 5,
    });

    if (
      !payload.sub ||
      typeof payload.sub !== "string" ||
      !(payload as AuthTokenPayload).sessionId ||
      typeof (payload as AuthTokenPayload).sessionId !== "string"
    ) {
      return null;
    }

    return payload as AuthTokenPayload;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

async function generateAuthToken(
  payload: Omit<AuthTokenPayload, "iss" | "aud" | "iat" | "exp">
): Promise<string> {
  return new SignJWT({ ...payload })
    .setProtectedHeader({ alg: "HS256" })
    .setIssuedAt()
    .setIssuer(TOKEN_ISSUER)
    .setAudience(TOKEN_AUDIENCE)
    .setExpirationTime("15m")
    .setSubject(payload.sub)
    .sign(getSecretKey());
}

function isPublicPath(pathname: string): boolean {
  if (PUBLIC_PATHS.has(pathname)) {
    return true;
  }

  for (const prefix of STATIC_ASSET_PREFIXES) {
    if (pathname.startsWith(prefix)) {
      return true;
    }
  }

  return false;
}

function getClientIdentifier(request: NextRequest): string {
  // Use a combination of IP and user agent for rate limiting
  const forwarded = request.headers.get("x-forwarded-for");
  const ip = forwarded ? forwarded.split(",")[0].trim() : "unknown";
  // Hash-like identifier without exposing raw IP
  return `${ip}`;
}

function checkRateLimit(clientId: string): {
  allowed: boolean;
  remaining: number;
  resetTime: number;
} {
  const now = Date.now();
  const entry = rateLimitStore.get(clientId);

  if (!entry || now > entry.resetTime) {
    const resetTime = now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS;
    rateLimitStore.set(clientId, { count: 1, resetTime });
    return {
      allowed: true,
      remaining: RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS - 1,
      resetTime,
    };
  }

  entry.count++;

  if (entry.count > RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS) {
    return { allowed: false, remaining: 0, resetTime: entry.resetTime };
  }

  return {
    allowed: true,
    remaining: RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS - entry.count,
    resetTime: entry.resetTime,
  };
}

function cleanupRateLimitStore(): void {
  const now = Date.now();
  for (const [key, entry] of rateLimitStore) {
    if (now > entry.resetTime) {
      rateLimitStore.delete(key);
    }
  }
}

function addSecurityHeaders(response: NextResponse): NextResponse {
  response.headers.set("X-Content-Type-Options", "nosniff");
  response.headers.set("X-Frame-Options", "DENY");
  response.headers.set("X-XSS-Protection", "1; mode=block");
  response.headers.set("Referrer-Policy", "strict-origin-when-cross-origin");
  response.headers.set(
    "Permissions-Policy",
    "camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()"
  );
  response.headers.set(
    "Strict-Transport-Security",
    "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload"
  );
  response.headers.set(
    "Content-Security-Policy",
    "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:; font-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'"
  );

  // Remove server identification headers
  response.headers.delete("X-Powered-By");
  response.headers.delete("Server");

  return response;
}

function createUnauthorizedResponse(
  request: NextRequest,
  message: string = "Unauthorized"
): NextResponse {
  const isApiRoute = request.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith("/api/");

  if (isApiRoute) {
    const response = NextResponse.json(
      { error: message },
      { status: 401 }
    );
    response.headers.set("WWW-Authenticate", 'Bearer realm="api"');
    return addSecurityHeaders(response);
  }

  // For page routes, redirect to login
  const loginUrl = new URL(LOGIN_PATH, request.url);
  // Sanitize the redirect path to prevent open redirect
  const redirectPath = request.nextUrl.pathname;
  if (
    redirectPath.startsWith("/") &&
    !redirectPath.startsWith("//") &&
    !redirectPath.includes("://")
  ) {
    loginUrl.searchParams.set("redirect", redirectPath);
  }

  const response = NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl);

  // Clear any invalid auth cookies
  response.cookies.delete(AUTH_COOKIE_NAME);

  return addSecurityHeaders(response);
}

function createRateLimitResponse(): NextResponse {
  const response = NextResponse.json(
    { error: "Too many requests. Please try again later." },
    { status: 429 }
  );
  response.headers.set("Retry-After", "60");
  return addSecurityHeaders(response);
}

function setAuthCookie(response: NextResponse, token: string): void {
  response.cookies.set(AUTH_COOKIE_NAME, token, {
    httpOnly: true,
    secure: true,
    sameSite: "strict",
    path: "/",
    maxAge: 900, // 15 minutes
  });
}

export default async function middleware(
  request: NextRequest
): Promise<NextResponse> {
  // Periodic cleanup of rate limit store
  if (Math.random() < 0.01) {
    cleanupRateLimitStore();
  }

  const pathname = request.nextUrl.pathname;

  // Validate the request URL to prevent path traversal
  const normalizedPath = pathname.replace(/\/+/g, "/");
  if (normalizedPath !== pathname || pathname.includes("..")) {
    return addSecurityHeaders(
      NextResponse.json({ error: "Invalid request path" }, { status: 400 })
    );
  }

  // Rate limiting
  const clientId = getClientIdentifier(request);
  const rateLimit = checkRateLimit(clientId);

  if (!rateLimit.allowed) {
    return createRateLimitResponse();
  }

  // Allow public paths without authentication
  if (isPublicPath(pathname)) {
    const response = NextResponse.next();
    response.headers.set(
      "X-RateLimit-Remaining",
      String(rateLimit.remaining)
    );
    return addSecurityHeaders(response);
  }

  // Extract auth token from cookie (not from Authorization header to prevent CSRF)
  const authToken = request.cookies.get(AUTH_COOKIE_NAME)?.value;
  const refreshToken = request.cookies.get(REFRESH_COOKIE_NAME)?.value;

  // No auth token present
  if (!authToken) {
    // Try refresh token
    if (refreshToken) {
      const refreshPayload = await verifyRefreshToken(refreshToken);
      if (refreshPayload) {
        // Issue new auth token
        try {
          const newAuthToken = await generateAuthToken({
            sub: refreshPayload.sub!,
            email: refreshPayload.email,
            role: refreshPayload.role,
            sessionId: refreshPayload.sessionId,
          });

          const response = NextResponse.next();
          setAuthCookie(response, newAuthToken);

          // Set user context headers for downstream use
          response.headers.set("X-User-Id", refreshPayload.sub!);
          response.headers.set("X-User-Role", refreshPayload.role);
          response.headers.set("X-Session-Id", refreshPayload.sessionId);
          response.headers.set(
            "X-RateLimit-Remaining",
            String(rateLimit.remaining)
          );

          return addSecurityHeaders(response);
        } catch {
          return createUnauthorizedResponse(request, "Token refresh failed");
        }
      }
    }

    return createUnauthorizedResponse(request);
  }

  // Verify the auth token
  const payload = await verifyAuthToken(authToken);

  if (!payload) {
    // Auth token invalid, try refresh
    if (refreshToken) {
      const refreshPayload = await verifyRefreshToken(refreshToken);
      if (refreshPayload) {
        try {
          const newAuthToken = await generateAuthToken({
            sub: refreshPayload.sub!,
            email: refreshPayload.email,
            role: refreshPayload.role,
            sessionId: refreshPayload.sessionId,
          });

          const response = NextResponse.next();
          setAuthCookie(response, newAuthToken);

          response.headers.set("X-User-Id", refreshPayload.sub!);
          response.headers.set("X-User-Role", refreshPayload.role);
          response.headers.set("X-Session-Id", refreshPayload.sessionId);
          response.headers.set(
            "X-RateLimit-Remaining",
            String(rateLimit.remaining)
          );

          return addSecurityHeaders(response);
        } catch {
          return createUnauthorizedResponse(request, "Token refresh failed");
        }
      }
    }

    return createUnauthorizedResponse(request, "Invalid or expired token");
  }

  // Check role-based access for admin routes
  if (pathname.startsWith("/admin") || pathname.startsWith("/api/admin")) {
    if (payload.role !== "admin") {
      const response = NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Forbidden: Insufficient permissions" },
        { status: 403 }
      );
      return addSecurityHeaders(response);
    }
  }

  // Check role-based access for moderator routes
  if (
    pathname.startsWith("/moderate") ||
    pathname.startsWith("/api/moderate")
  ) {
    const allowedRoles = new Set(["admin", "moderator"]);
    if (!allowedRoles.has(payload.role)) {
      const response = NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Forbidden: Insufficient permissions" },
        { status: 403 }
      );
      return addSecurityHeaders(response);
    }
  }

  // Token is valid - proceed with request
  const response = NextResponse.next();

  // Set user context headers for downstream API routes / server components
  // These headers are set by the middleware and should be trusted by downstream code
  // since they come from verified JWT claims
  response.headers.set("X-User-Id", payload.sub!);
  response.headers.set("X-User-Email", payload.email);
  response.headers.set("X-User-Role", payload.role);
  response.headers.set("X-Session-Id", payload.sessionId);
  response.headers.set("X-RateLimit-Remaining", String(rateLimit.remaining));

  // Proactive token refresh: if token expires within 5 minutes, issue a new one
  if (payload.exp) {
    const timeUntilExpiry = payload.exp * 1000 - Date.now();
    if (timeUntilExpiry < 5 * 60 * 1000) {
      try {
        const newToken = await generateAuthToken({
          sub: payload.sub!,
          email: payload.email,
          role: payload.role,
          sessionId: payload.sessionId,
        });
        setAuthCookie(response, newToken);
      } catch {
        // If token refresh fails, continue with existing valid token
        // It will be refreshed on the next request
      }
    }
  }

  return addSecurityHeaders(response);
}

export const config = {
  matcher: [
    /*
     * Match all request paths except:
     * - _next/static (static files)
     * - _next/image (image optimization files)
     * - favicon.ico (favicon file)
     * - public folder files
     */
    "/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|public/).*)",
  ],
};
